questions.question: The diagram shows the human lungs and associated structures.

parts.question: Complete the diagram by naming the labelled parts.
parts.answer: clockwise from right hand side: trachea / windpipe; bronchiole; larynx / voice box

parts.question: Explain why a human needs lungs.
parts.answer: gaseous / gas exchange; oxygen in + carbon dioxide out; oxygen for respiration / carbon dioxide from respiration; energy from respiration used for movement / relevant activity; needs a large surface area / moist surface / blood capillaries close to air / thin membrane

questions.question: A farmer is growing a crop of tomato plants.

parts.question: The tomato plants carry out photosynthesis. Describe the process of photosynthesis.
parts.answer: light (energy) absorbed / captured; chlorophyll / chloroplasts; water + carbon dioxide are needed; glucose / carbohydrate / sugar / starch produced; oxygen produced

parts.question: The farmer grows the tomato plants in a glasshouse instead of in an open field. Suggest reasons why this is an advantage to the farmer.
parts.answer: more / optimum + growth / tomatoes / yield / photosynthesis; higher / optimum temperature; glass traps heat; for enzymes; protects from bad weather / example; protects from insect / pests / plant pathogens; carbon dioxide / light intensity / wavelength + controlled

parts.question: Suggest one disadvantage, other than cost, of growing plants in this way.
parts.answer: have to water / no rain; increase + pathogens / disease; pollination may be difficult

parts.question: The graph shows how the rate of photosynthesis can vary with light intensity when all other environmental conditions are kept the same. Explain how this graph shows that light intensity is the limiting factor for photosynthesis at light intensity P but not at light intensity Q.
parts.answer: at P increase light intensity + up (photosynthesis / rate / gradient); at Q increase in light intensity + no change (photosynthesis / rate / gradient)

parts.question: Name one environmental condition that could be limiting photosynthesis at light intensity Q.
parts.answer: carbon dioxide; temperature; water / precipitation

questions.question: A man is cooking in a hot kitchen and sweat is being released by sweat glands in his skin.

parts.question: Explain the term homeostasis with reference to sweating.
parts.answer: (homeostasis is) the maintenance of a constant; internal environment / temperature; return to norm / return to set-point / negative feedback; evaporation of sweat removes heat / thermal energy / cools / decreases temperature

parts.question: Explain how receptors and blood vessels in the man's skin are also involved in homeostasis when he is in the hot kitchen.
parts.answer: receptors + detection of temperature; impulse to brain / CNS; vasodilation; arterioles / blood vessels widen; increase in blood + near skin surface; greater + loss of heat / thermal energy; by radiation

parts.question: Sweat contains very small quantities of the waste product urea. State the name of the organ where most urea is made in the body.
parts.answer: liver

parts.question: Use the data in the table to compare the chemical composition of urine and blood plasma.
parts.answer: less urea in blood plasma; more sodium in blood plasma; less chloride in blood plasma; comparative data quote with units; data manipulation, e.g. 50 × higher urea in urine than blood plasma

parts.question: A patient has kidney disease and is about to start dialysis treatment. Suggest and explain the concentration of urea in the sweat of the patient compared to that of a person who does not have kidney disease.
parts.answer: high(er) / over 22 (in patient); reduced filtration / reduced removal of urea (from kidney); reference to blood plasma

questions.question: The kapok tree is a flowering plant that grows rapidly to become one of the tallest trees in a tropical rainforest ecosystem.

parts.question: Use the information provided to draw a food chain for this tropical rainforest in the space provided. The chain should contain three organisms.
parts.answer: kapok / tree + insect + frog / bird; arrow in correct direction from kapok / tree / leaves / plant at start

parts.question: Suggest and explain ways in which being tall may be helpful for the survival of this tree species.
parts.answer: gets light / reduced + competition for light; photosynthesis; exposed to wind; for seed dispersal; easier for bats to find flowers

parts.question: Explain why the bacteria and fungi found in the rainforest soil are important in this ecosystem.
parts.answer: decomposition / decay / decomposers / break down (organic matter); use of enzymes; carbon / nitrogen / nutrients + recycled / returned to soil; carbon / nitrogen / named nutrient used by plants

parts.question: Name one substance, found in plants but not in animals, that contains carbon.
parts.answer: starch / cellulose / chlorophyll / sucrose

parts.question: Explain how burning trees contributes to global warming.
parts.answer: releases carbon dioxide / carbon dioxide into the atmosphere; greenhouse gas; traps thermal energy / causes temperature to increase; removal of carbon sink

questions.question: The graph shows how blood alcohol concentration changes after a person drinks alcohol.

parts.question: Suggest how the shape of the graph can be explained by what happens in parts of the body after drinking alcohol.
parts.answer: increases / in first hour + absorption / enters blood; in stomach/small intestine / duodenum / ileum; hepatic portal vein; liver; decrease in alcohol + break down / excretion / sweat / urine; using an enzyme

parts.question: The graph shows that the highest blood alcohol concentration for this person after drinking alcohol is 0.75 g per dm3. Suggest two reasons why the highest blood alcohol concentration may be different for two people after drinking the same volume of alcohol.
parts.answer: gender; weight / mass / size; how much eaten/different rate of absorption; age; different + ability to break down alcohol / health of liver / metabolism; kidney efficiency

parts.question: Describe the short-term effects of a high blood alcohol concentration.
parts.answer: negative effect on reaction time / responses; depressant; blurred vision; slurred speech; reduced coordination / balance / dizziness; description of cognitive ability impairment, e.g. loss of judgement, false courage, antisocial behaviour, increased aggression, drowsiness

questions.question: Explain the 'lock and key' hypothesis of enzyme action using a named example.

parts.question: Explain the 'lock and key' hypothesis of enzyme action using a named example.
parts.answer: enzyme + name, e.g. amylase; substrate + name, e.g. amylose/starch; enzyme + has an active site; specificity of enzyme; substrate fits into active site / enzyme-substrate complex formed; named product formed; enzyme unchanged at end of reaction

parts.question: Explain, using examples, why proteins are essential for all living organisms.
parts.answer: growth / new cells; repair; energy in absence of carbohydrates/fats; muscle; hair / nails / skin; hormones; enzymes; antibodies; named protein, e.g. haemoglobin, fibrinogen; cell membrane / carrier protein; blood clotting

questions.question: Explain the role of the nucleus and cell division in the inheritance of characteristics from parents to offspring.

parts.question: Explain the role of the nucleus and cell division in the inheritance of characteristics from parents to offspring.
parts.answer: chromosomes; DNA / genetic material; genes / alleles; code + for proteins; proteins determine characteristics; meiosis / meiotic division / reduction division / produces haploid cells / produces gametes; fertilisation / fertilisation described; combination/mix + of genetic material / genes / chromosomes / DNA / characteristics

parts.question: If people are exposed to ionising radiation, this may affect the inheritance of characteristics by their children. Suggest how this exposure to radiation may affect inheritance of characteristics.
parts.answer: increases the chance / probability of; mutation; change in chromosome number; change in gene structure / sequence; codes for new/different/incorrect protein; may result in new beneficial variation; may result in genetic disease / example of genetic condition

questions.question: Water enters living organisms and is transported to all their cells. Compare the transport of water in a human and a flowering plant.

parts.question: Compare the transport of water in a human and a flowering plant.
parts.answer: similarities both carried in tubes; one way movement; both travel between cells by osmosis; differences plants from soil / root; humans by ingestion / alimentary canal part; plants in xylem; humans in blood vessels; detail of xylem vessel, e.g. no end-walls, lignin; detail of blood vessel, e.g. muscular walls elastic walls of artery; Transpiration; heart pumps; detail of mechanism, e.g. cohesion / adhesion / continuous water column; lost through evaporation; detail of mechanism, e.g. heart muscles contract, elastic tissue allows pulsing of blood; lost through sweating / urination

parts.question: Water in humans and flowering plants contains dissolved mineral ions. Suggest reasons why the mineral ion requirements of humans and plants are different.
parts.answer: magnesium + for chlorophyll; iron + haemoglobin / blood; calcium + bones; calcium + cell walls; deficiency in plants, e.g. yellow leaves; deficiency in animals, e.g. rickets, anaemia; idea that have different cell types / structures / physiology in organism; produce + different chemicals / chemicals in different proportions

questions.question: Sperm cells and pollen grains are both involved in sexual reproduction. Compare the functions of a sperm cell and a pollen grain and describe how the structure of each is related to its function.

parts.question: Compare the functions of a sperm cell and a pollen grain and describe how the structure of each is related to its function.
parts.answer: similarities both gametes; haploid / half chromosome number of body cell / produced by meiosis; fuses with egg / fertilisation / to produce zygote; differences sperm cell has flagellum / tail + move; enzymes / acrosome + to penetrate egg; wind pollination / carried by wind; insect pollination / carried by insect; small / light / 'sticky' / sculptured coat; pollen tube + to reach egg / ovule / ovary

parts.question: Suggest reasons why sexual reproduction may be better for the survival of a species than asexual reproduction.
parts.answer: sexual reproduction + genetically different offspring; sexual reproduction + variation; asexual reproduction + genetically identical offspring/clones; reference to mitosis / meiosis; condition that affects survival, e.g. disease / temperature / water availability; some individuals will survive changing environmental condition; live in wider range of environments
